name: Short Gen
shortDesc: AI Shorts factory. Pulls a trending prompt from r/WritingPrompts, has GPT-4o write a 10-scene storyboard, generates a DALL-E image and TTS narration per scene, stitches it into a vertical MP4, and uploads it to YouTube. Unattended content pipeline from one Reddit post to one Short.

hero.title: Reddit prompt in,
paragraphs: Making AI-generated Shorts is a stack of small annoying steps. Pick a prompt, script it, draft scenes, generate art, record narration, edit, render vertical, write a title, upload, tag. Each step is doable, but doing all of them every day is the part that breaks.

Short Gen is a Python pipeline that automates every one of those steps. It pulls the top post of the day from r/WritingPrompts, has GPT-4o write a strict-JSON 10-scene storyboard, generates a DALL-E-3 image and OpenAI TTS narration for each scene, stitches the scenes into a 1080x1920 MP4 with MoviePy, and resumable-uploads the result to YouTube via the Data API. One command, one Short.

points.title: GPT-4o storyboards in strict JSON
points.desc: `agent.py` prompts GPT-4o for a 10-scene storyboard with 15-20 word narrations and 0-2 characters per scene, returned as parseable JSON (no markdown). Separate calls generate reference images for characters and settings so the look stays consistent across scenes.

points.title: DALL-E-3 + TTS per scene
points.desc: Each scene gets a cinematic Pixar-style 3D illustration from DALL-E-3 and narrated audio from OpenAI TTS using the `onyx` voice. Output lands as `image.png` and `audio.mp3` in a scene folder, ready for assembly.

points.title: MoviePy stitches the vertical
points.desc: `movie.py` center-crops each image to 1080x1920, binds it to the narration, exports `scene{N}.mp4`, then concatenates the scenes into a single `short.mp4`. No editor, no manual timeline.

points.title: Resumable upload to YouTube
points.desc: `youtube.py` handles the OAuth2 token refresh and uses the YouTube Data API v3 resumable-upload flow to publish the finished Short with title, tags (`#AI #Shorts #AIShorts`), and category 24.

steps.title: Pull a prompt
steps.desc: `reddit.py` uses `praw` to grab the top post of the day from r/WritingPrompts. The post title becomes the seed for the story.

steps.title: Plan the story
steps.desc: GPT-4o turns the prompt into a 10-scene storyboard JSON: arc from setup to resolution, scene narrations capped at 15-20 words, character and setting refs for visual consistency. Saved to `story/storyboard.json`.

steps.title: Generate art and narration
steps.desc: For each scene the agent fires a DALL-E-3 image request and a TTS request in sequence. Output goes to `scenes/scene{N}/image.png` and `scenes/scene{N}/audio.mp3`.

steps.title: Render and upload
steps.desc: MoviePy builds each `scene{N}.mp4`, concatenates into `short.mp4`, and `youtube.py` runs the OAuth resumable upload to publish public with the AI Shorts tags applied.

tech.label: Under the hood
tech.title: How it's built
tech.subtitle: A handful of small Python files, each owning one step of the pipeline.

items: `workflow.py`, the entry point that wires the steps together
`filesystem.py`, builds ordered scene/clip path lists so each stage knows what to read and write
OpenAI SDK with GPT-4o for storyboard generation, strict JSON output (no markdown wrappers)
DALL-E-3 for scene art plus separate character/setting reference images for visual consistency
OpenAI TTS with the `onyx` voice for narration
`praw` for Reddit (top post of day from r/WritingPrompts)
`moviepy` + `PIL` for center-crop to 1080x1920, audio binding, per-scene render, concatenation
`google-api-python-client` OAuth2 flow for YouTube Data API v3 resumable uploads, category 24, public, tagged `#AI #Shorts #AIShorts`
Captions and Ken Burns motion on still images (README backlog)
Veo2 for true motion clips per scene
Agent verification loops to retry scenes that fail visual or narrative checks
